pin.contextualHelpBody:
The **PIN** code consists of 8 digits:
• The first 4 digits were provided to you at the registry office;  
• The last 4 digits were mailed to your home along with your electronic ID card.  
  
Combine them and enter them in this order.  
The PUK code, on the other hand, is needed to unlock the **PIN** after three failed attempts. It consists of 8 digits and is issued along with the **PIN**.

idp_login.contextualHelpContent:
The password is the alphanumeric code you chose when you created your SPID Digital Identity.
If you don’t remember it, you can recover it or set a new password by following the instructions provided by your Identity Provider.  
Once you’ve entered the required information, you’ll receive an OTP code from your Identity Provider. Confirm the login request and return to the IO app to complete the login process. You will then see a screen authorizing the Identity Provider to send IO a set of information necessary to enable you to use the app and the various services offered by public agencies through IO. Tap “I agree” to give your consent and proceed with authentication.

landing.contextualHelpContent:
To access the IO app, you’ll need to sign in using your SPID (Public Digital Identity System) credentials or your CIE (Electronic ID Card).
**Thanks to these authentication methods, your access to the app will always be secure.**  
To apply for a SPID digital identity, visit [spid.gov.it](https://www.spid.gov.it) and choose an Identity Provider from the list provided. 
If you’re interested in applying for the CIE, you can find more information on your municipality’s website and schedule an appointment.

1.content: SPID is the authentication system that allows citizens and businesses to access online services provided by the public administration and participating private entities using a single digital identity. The SPID identity consists of credentials (username and password) that are issued to the user and enable access to all online services.
1.title: What is SPID?

2.content: To obtain your SPID credentials, you must contact Aruba, EtnaHiTech, Infocamere, Infocert, Namirial, Poste, Register, Sielte, Tim, Lepida, or TeamSystem. These entities (known as identity providers) offer various ways to apply for and obtain SPID; you can choose the one that best suits your needs. Find all the information on where and how to request your SPID credentials on the website [https://www.spid.gov.it/cos-e-spid/come-attivare-spid/](https://www.spid.gov.it/cos-e-spid/come-attivare-spid/).
2.title: How can you get SPID?

3.content: Don't worry: you can retrieve them at any time. Select your Identity Provider to find out how to proceed: [Aruba](https://guide.pec.it/spid/recupero-dati/procedure-di-recupero-dati-smarriti.aspx), [EtnaID](https://etnaid.eht.eu/signup/forgotPassword), [Infocamere](https://selfcarespid.infocamere.it/spid-selfCare/#RecoveryEmergencyCode), [Infocert](https://my.infocert.it/selfcare/#/recoveryPin), [Namirial](https://portale.namirialtsp.com/private/user/spid_reset.php), [Poste](https://posteid.poste.it/recuperocredenziali.shtml), [Register, if you’ve forgotten your username](https://spid.register.it/selfcare/recovery/username), [Register, if you’ve forgotten your password](https://spid.register.it/selfcare/recovery/password), [Sielte, if you forgot your password](https://myid.sieltecloud.it/profile/recovery/forgotPassword), [Tim, if you forgot your username](https://login.id.tim.it/mps/fu.php), [Tim, if you forgot your password](https://login.id.tim.it/mps/fp.php), [Lepida](https://id.lepida.it/idm/app/recupero_credenziali.jsp), [TeamSystem, if you've forgotten your username](https://spid.teamsystem.com/it/auth/usernameLost), [TeamSystem, if you've forgotten your password](https://spid.teamsystem.com/it/auth/passwordLost).
3.title: Lost your SPID credentials?

4.content: The CIE, or Electronic Identity Card, is a highly secure electronic document equipped with a radio-frequency microprocessor that allows it to be used in a variety of situations, including access to online services. It meets our country’s security requirements and replaces the paper version.
4.title: What is the CIE?

5.content: You can go to [https://www.prenotazionicie.interno.gov.it/](https://www.prenotazionicie.interno.gov.it) and enter the name of your municipality to check whether it uses the Agenda CIE online appointment system. If so, you can book an appointment directly on the website without having to log in. Alternatively, you can request an appointment online through your municipality’s website or by visiting one of the municipal offices.
5.title: How can you get the CIE?

6.content: SPID identity is issued by Digital Identity Providers (Identity Providers), private entities accredited by AgID that, in accordance with the rules issued by the Agency, provide digital identities and manage user authentication. Your Identity Provider is the entity you have chosen to provide your SPID identity.
6.title: What is an Identity Provider?

7.title: How to choose an Identity Provider?

8.content: Yes, it is possible to apply for more than one digital identity—even with different security levels—and to choose from different digital identity providers. If a citizen has multiple digital identities, they are free to log in to IO using whichever SPID credentials they prefer.
8.title: Is it possible to have more than one digital identity?

card.content:
The CIE is the Electronic ID Card introduced in Italy to replace the traditional paper ID card.

It’s useful to have one for:
 * Accessing online government services (e.g., INPS, Agenzia delle Entrate)
 * Officially identifying yourself in Italy and abroad
 * Traveling to EU countries and other countries that accept it in place of a passport.
card.title: Electronic Identity Card (CIE)

pin.content:
The CIE PIN is an 8-digit code that was provided to you in two separate instances:
 * You received the first 4 digits when you applied for the CIE
 * You received the last 4 digits along with the CIE.

messages.contextualHelpContent:
**The Messages section contains all incoming communications from institutions that offer their services through IO**. 
You can set reminders for deadlines or pay a bill directly from the message.  
Messages are divided into **Received** (which contains all messages that have arrived and have not been archived), **Deadlines** (which displays only messages containing a due date, to help you manage your to-do list), and **Archived** (which keeps track of all archived messages).  
To archive a message, simply press and hold the message you want to archive, and select the “Archive” button that will appear on the screen.

unlockCode.contextualHelpContent:
**The unlock code is the security code that keeps your data secure in the IO app, and you set it yourself the first time you log in**.   
If this is your first time logging in to the app, choose any numeric code you like and make sure to remember it so you can use it every time you log in.  
If you’ve already set it up and can’t remember it, you can select “Forgot unlock code” and set a new one.  
The unlock code is tied to the device on which you set it: if you change phones, you’ll need to set it again the first time you log in to the IO app.

rooted.body: If you tap “Continue,” you agree to proceed at your own risk; otherwise, close the app.
rooted.bodyAndroid:
**Root permissions** are enabled on your device. This happens when the operating system is modified or installed incorrectly. This could compromise the integrity of the system, and the IO app may not function properly.

**What should you do?**

We recommend using the IO app on a different device with an intact and up-to-date operating system.

If you continue, you agree to proceed at your own risk when interacting with the services offered. A lack of integrity may lead to data theft or other fraudulent activities. You also agree that the app developer is in no way liable for any damages, losses, or consequences caused by the IO app under these operating conditions.
rooted.bodyiOS:
Your device has been **jailbroken**. This happens when the operating system is modified or installed incorrectly. This could compromise the integrity of the system, and the IO app may not work properly.

**What should you do?**

We recommend using the IO app on a different device with an intact and up-to-date operating system.

If you continue, you agree to proceed at your own risk when interacting with the services offered. A lack of integrity may lead to data theft or other fraudulent activities. You also agree that the app developer is in no way liable for any damages, losses, or consequences caused by the IO app under these operating conditions.

services.contextualHelpContent:
**In the Services section, you can view the list of agencies that offer their services on IO, as well as the list of services associated with each institution.** Services are divided into local services—where you can select institution of interest based on the geographic area where you live—national services—that is, central government institution offering services to all Italian citizens—and finally, all services, where you can find the complete list of all services available on IO (both local and national).   
We invite you to use this section to explore the services made available by IO, set up your list of local services, and view the details of individual services. Whenever a new local or national service is added to IO, you’ll receive a notification so you can immediately check what it is and whether it’s useful to you.

QRtoPay.contextualHelpContent: **Here you can make a voluntary payment for a notice using pagoPA**, the payment platform for the public administration. You can pay using a paper notice by scanning the QR code on the notice and then completing the payment directly through the app. You can also upload a QR code from your phone’s gallery or manually enter the notice’s identification details (Notice Code).

wallet.contextualHelpContent:
**The "Wallet" section lists your payment methods, your bonuses and discount cards, and the history of transactions made with pagoPA**, the payment platform for the Public Administration. 
Here, you can also pay all paper payment notices bearing the pagoPA logo: scan the QR code on the paper notice and complete the payment directly from the app. IO currently supports only credit/debit card payments, but other payment methods will be added soon.

amountInfo.message:
When the amount changes, it means it has been updated automatically.

If it is higher, there may be interest, penalties, or notification costs.

If the pagoPA notice concerns a legally binding communication, the increase in the amount due may depend on several factors, for example:
- whether you received the notice via registered mail (the printing and mailing costs of which may be charged to the recipient); 
- the number of delivery attempts. 
For more details, contact the payee.

bottomSheet.description:
**Credit or Debit Card**

Please check with your bank. The most common reasons are:

1. Your card is not enabled for online purchases.
2. You have not yet activated the 3DS service: this is a security system for online payments.
3. Your card has been suspended or blocked.
4. You have “paused” your card.


**Other Payment Methods**

Contact the customer support for your payment method and ask for the reason for the decline.
